key_points: Every state in India has a Legislative Assembly where representatives are elected by the people.
MLA stands for Member of Legislative Assembly; they are elected by the people of a specific constituency.
A constituency is a particular area from which all the voters living there choose their representatives.
The political party whose MLAs have won more than half the number of constituencies in a state is called the 'Majority' party.
The Majority party forms the government, and all the other members are called the 'Opposition'.
examples: Chief Minister (CM): The leader of the ruling party who is chosen by the elected MLAs.
Governor: The head of the State who is appointed by the Central Government to ensure the state follows the Constitution.
Cabinet Ministers: MLAs who are given specific departments like Health, Education, or Agriculture by the CM.
Press Conference: A meeting where ministers explain government steps to reporters and the public.
formulas: State Government = Chief Minister + Council of Ministers
exam_tips: Remember that every MLA belongs to a political party, even if they are 'Independent'.
Note that the Legislative Assembly is the place where all MLAs (ruling and opposition) meet to discuss various things.
Understand that the CM and other ministers have the 'Executive' power to run the government.
Identify that 'Coalition' occurs when no single party gets a majority and two or more parties join together.
